reason: Figure: AND(x,y) OR'd with NAND(x',y). f = xy + (x'y)' = xy + x + y' = x + y'. Truth table check: 00->1, 01->0, 10->1, 11->1 = x + y'.
Two processes each waiting on the other is the textbook definition of deadlock (circular wait).
Transport layer provides reliable end-to-end delivery (TCP: ack, retransmit, sequencing).
Branch / control-flow instructions alter the program counter and thus program flow.
Push/pop are the LIFO stack primitives.
Class A uses /8, i.e. 255.0.0.0.
+9 = 00001001; invert -> 11110110; +1 -> 11110111. Note option B (11110110) is the 1's complement, a classic distractor.
FDDI is a dual counter-rotating token ring.
Physical layer converts bits into electrical/optical/electromagnetic signals on the medium.
Transport = end-to-end (process-to-process) delivery; node-to-node is data link, routing tables are network layer.

Network virtual terminal, file transfer/access/management, and mail service are ALL standard application-layer services (Forouzan's list), so 'All of the above' is correct; keying only 'Mail service' is wrong.

unanswerable - options A and D are literally identical ('Fiber Distributed Data Interface'), so two labels are equally correct. The expansion itself is Fiber Distributed Data Interface; option D is a transcription defect and must be fixed.

A single processor executes only one process at a given instant (A true), but B is false (multiprogramming preempts before completion) and C is false (a single CPU is exactly where scheduling is needed), so 'All of the above' cannot hold.
Blocked/waiting state holds processes waiting on I/O or an event; waiting for the processor is the Ready state.
Process states are new/ready/running/blocked/terminated; 'Privileged' is a CPU mode, not a process state.
Moore outputs are a function of the current state only; Mealy depends on state and input.
Running several programs concurrently is multitasking.
Big-O is an asymptotic upper bound, conventionally used to express worst-case complexity (Omega = best, Theta = tight).
F = x + y'z: F=1 whenever x=1 (rows 100-111) or y=0,z=1 (row 001); all other rows 0.
Traced figure: three 3-input ANDs feeding an OR -> f = x1'x2x3' + x1x2x3' + x1x2x3 = x2(x1 + x3'); ones only at 010, 110, 111.
